India.narrative: After the 1965 Immigration Act ended national-origin quotas, Indian immigration accelerated through family reunification and skilled-worker visas.

China.narrative: Chinese-American communities expanded as immigration policy opened and diplomatic relations evolved.

Mexico.narrative: Mexican migration has deep regional roots, with major policy shifts in 1965 and 1986 reform.

Ireland.narrative: Irish migration peaked during the Great Famine era and early 20th century, before quota restrictions.

Italy.narrative: Italian immigrants arrived in large numbers through Ellis Island before the 1924 quota system.

England.narrative: English settlement shaped colonial America long before modern immigration law.

Scotland.narrative: Scottish immigrants often entered through mid-Atlantic ports and Appalachian settlements.

Japan.narrative: Japanese-American history includes early labor migration, internment, and post-1965 renewal.

South Korea.narrative: Korean immigration grew after 1965, with additional waves tied to political and economic change.

default.narrative: Post-1965 immigration policy reshaped who could enter the United States and where families settled.
